items.briefing:
A cyberattack on Mackay Sugar has raised concerns beyond the company itself, with experts warning it highlights vulnerabilities across Australia's food supply chain. Separately, a Clayton Utz report is urging Australian boards to better manage third-party supplier risks and gaps in how AI is being rolled out — both areas where many organisations remain exposed. From 1 July, some text messages will look different as new sender ID protections roll out, which is a positive step against scam texts.

A fileless malware called Phantom Stealer is also actively targeting saved browser passwords and credentials. **Practical tip:** Avoid saving passwords in your browser — use a dedicated password manager instead, and enable multi-factor authentication on your important accounts.

featured.title: Mackay Sugar cyber attack flagged as broader risk to Australia’s food supply chain
featured.quote: Cyber attack on Australia's second-largest sugar exporter highlights critical vulnerability in national food supply chain.
